schema_name: Radioactive Waste Management RMS Ideal Multi-Table Schema
purpose: Comprehensive reference schema for building a Requirement Management System (RMS) for radioactive waste management. This package is intended to support later database creation, metadata governance, seed loading, validation rules, and traceability design.
design_principles: Requirements are managed as controlled records, not as simple text items.
Traceability must exist from external needs to implementation evidence.
Requirement hierarchy alone is not sufficient; relationship networks must also be supported.
Functional architecture, constraints, decisions, verification, change control, documents, and baselines are modeled as separate but linked entities.
Revision history must be preserved and never overwritten.
Documents and requirements are different objects and must be modeled separately.
The schema is intentionally normalized enough to support long-term nuclear lifecycle use.

tables.table_purpose: Controlled lookup table defining the standard requirement level model used by the RMS.

fields.name: level_code
fields.description: Unique level identifier such as L0, L1, L2a, L2b, L3a, L3b, L4a, L4b, L5, or L6.

fields.name: level_name
fields.description: Human-readable name of the level.

fields.name: short_definition
fields.description: Short definition of what this level means.

fields.name: detailed_explanation
fields.description: Long explanation of what content belongs to the level.

fields.name: typical_question_answered
fields.description: Typical question that the level helps answer.

fields.name: typical_sources
fields.description: Typical source categories for the level.

fields.name: expected_content
fields.description: Typical content types expected at this level.

fields.name: example_requirements
fields.description: Illustrative example requirements for the level.

fields.name: database_hint
fields.description: Guidance for using this level in database design.

fields.name: sort_order
fields.description: Display and processing order of the level.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Indicates whether the level is active for use.

tables.table_purpose: Core master table containing one record per controlled requirement.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: req_uid
fields.description: Stable immutable requirement identifier used across revisions and traceability chains.

fields.name: req_code
fields.description: Human-readable requirement code following the chosen coding convention.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Short title of the requirement.

fields.name: statement
fields.description: Approved requirement statement in controlled language.

fields.name: normalized_statement
fields.description: Normalized or cleaned version of the statement for searching or analytics.

fields.name: requirement_kind
fields.description: Type of requirement, such as need, goal, function, characteristic, performance target, design input, design output specification, production specification, verification requirement, validation requirement, or constraint-like requirement.

fields.name: level_code
fields.description: Foreign key to requirement_levels.level_code.

fields.name: domain_type
fields.description: Domain category such as needs_domain, functional_architecture_interface, solution_domain, or implementation_documentation.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Lifecycle status of the requirement such as draft, proposed, approved, implemented, verified, retired, or obsolete.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Relative priority such as critical, high, medium, or low.

fields.name: criticality
fields.description: Business or safety criticality of the requirement.

fields.name: safety_significance
fields.description: Safety significance class or grading information.

fields.name: security_class
fields.description: Information or security classification if applicable.

fields.name: lifecycle_phase_id
fields.description: Foreign key to lifecycle_phases.id indicating primary applicability phase.

fields.name: owner_org_id
fields.description: Foreign key to organizations.id indicating the owning organization.

fields.name: responsible_role_id
fields.description: Foreign key to roles.id indicating the primary responsible role.

fields.name: approval_state
fields.description: Approval state such as not_submitted, under_review, approved, conditionally_approved, or rejected.

fields.name: effective_from
fields.description: Date when the current requirement became effective.

fields.name: effective_to
fields.description: Date when the requirement ceased to be effective, if retired.

fields.name: current_revision_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirement_revisions.id for the currently approved revision snapshot.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Indicates whether the requirement is currently active.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp.

tables.table_purpose: Flexible extension table for structured requirement metadata that varies across facility types, waste streams, and project maturity.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id.

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Name of the attribute, such as waste_class, barrier_element, host_rock_parameter, retrievability_flag, temperature_limit, or licensing_relevance.

fields.name: attribute_type
fields.description: Data type classification such as text, number, boolean, date, enum, or reference.

fields.name: attribute_value_text
fields.description: Text value if the attribute is textual.

fields.name: attribute_value_number
fields.description: Numeric value if the attribute is numeric.

fields.name: attribute_value_unit
fields.description: Unit attached to a numeric value.

fields.name: attribute_value_date
fields.description: Date value if relevant.

fields.name: attribute_value_boolean
fields.description: Boolean value if relevant.

fields.name: source_revision_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirement_revisions.id indicating the revision from which the attribute value was taken or confirmed.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Optional explanatory note.

tables.table_purpose: Traceability table linking a requirement to one or more originating or supporting sources.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id.

fields.name: source_document_id
fields.description: Foreign key to documents.id.

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: Category of source, such as law, regulation, standard, policy, safety_case, design_report, stakeholder_input, internal_decision, contract, or R&D_output.

fields.name: source_locator
fields.description: Precise locator such as article, clause, paragraph, page, table, appendix, section, or figure number.

fields.name: quoted_text
fields.description: Optional extracted text from the source that directly supports the requirement.

fields.name: interpretation_note
fields.description: Interpretation or translation note explaining how the source became a requirement.

fields.name: is_primary_source
fields.description: True if this is the primary governing source for the requirement.

tables.table_purpose: Network table capturing non-hierarchical and hierarchical relationships between requirements.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: from_requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id representing the source side of the relationship.

fields.name: to_requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id representing the target side of the relationship.

fields.name: relationship_type
fields.description: Relationship category such as derives_from, decomposes_to, refines, constrains, conflicts_with, overlaps_with, duplicates, depends_on, supersedes, mitigates, justified_by, satisfied_by, verified_by, or validated_by.

fields.name: relationship_strength
fields.description: Optional strength or confidence descriptor such as strong, medium, weak, direct, or indirect.

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Rationale for why the relationship exists.

fields.name: created_in_change_id
fields.description: Foreign key to change_requests.id if the relationship was created or modified through formal change control.

tables.table_purpose: Functional architecture table defining the programme elements, facilities, subsystems, SSCs, processes, and organizational units to which requirements may be allocated.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: element_code
fields.description: Human-readable code for the system element.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Element name.

fields.name: element_type
fields.description: Type such as programme, facility, subsystem, SSC, barrier_element, process, activity, organization_unit, document_product, or decision_product.

fields.name: parent_element_id
fields.description: Self-reference to system_elements.id for hierarchy of architecture elements.

fields.name: lifecycle_phase_id
fields.description: Foreign key to lifecycle_phases.id indicating main phase relevance.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the element and its role in the architecture.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current status such as planned, active, decommissioned, retired, or conceptual.

tables.table_purpose: Table defining interfaces and dependencies between architecture elements.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: from_element_id
fields.description: Foreign key to system_elements.id for the source element.

fields.name: to_element_id
fields.description: Foreign key to system_elements.id for the target element.

fields.name: interface_type
fields.description: Interface category such as material_flow, information_flow, command_control, physical_connection, organizational_dependency, or schedule_dependency.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the interface and its governing conditions.

fields.name: governing_requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id for the requirement that governs the interface.

tables.table_purpose: Links requirements to functional architecture elements, showing where they are implemented, owned, or fulfilled.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id.

fields.name: allocated_to_element_id
fields.description: Foreign key to system_elements.id.

fields.name: allocation_type
fields.description: Allocation category such as primary, supporting, shared, inherited, or derived.

fields.name: allocated_function
fields.description: Function or role that the allocated element performs in satisfying the requirement.

fields.name: allocated_characteristic
fields.description: Characteristic or quality aspect being allocated.

fields.name: target_value
fields.description: Optional numeric target allocated to the element.

fields.name: target_unit
fields.description: Unit of the target value.

fields.name: allocation_rationale
fields.description: Explanation for the allocation decision.

fields.name: allocation_status
fields.description: Status such as proposed, accepted, implemented, or superseded.

tables.table_purpose: Controlled table for external or internal boundary conditions that constrain design, operations, records, or programme execution.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: constraint_code
fields.description: Human-readable constraint code.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Short title of the constraint.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Full statement of the limiting condition or assumption boundary.

fields.name: constraint_type
fields.description: Type such as waste_inventory, geology, site_boundary, licensed_decision, budget, schedule, political, societal, existing_facility, or environmental_condition.

fields.name: source_document_id
fields.description: Foreign key to documents.id for the source of the constraint.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Status such as active, superseded, under_review, or retired.

fields.name: owner_org_id
fields.description: Foreign key to organizations.id for the owning body.

fields.name: current_revision_id
fields.description: Revision reference if constraints are version-controlled separately.

tables.table_purpose: Table describing how a constraint affects requirements, system elements, or both.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: constraint_id
fields.description: Foreign key to constraints.id.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id if the impact is on a requirement.

fields.name: system_element_id
fields.description: Foreign key to system_elements.id if the impact is on an architecture element.

fields.name: impact_type
fields.description: Impact type such as limits, informs, blocks, modifies, conditions, or requires_decision.

fields.name: impact_description
fields.description: Description of the impact.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Optional impact severity such as high, medium, or low.

fields.name: decision_needed_flag
fields.description: Indicates whether a decision is needed to address the impact.

tables.table_purpose: Controlled lookup table of standard verification methods.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Method code such as REVIEW, ANALYSIS, INSPECTION, TEST, DEMONSTRATION, AUDIT, MODELLING, or MONITORING.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Method name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Definition and usage note for the method.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Indicates whether the method is currently active for use.

tables.table_purpose: Formal verification records stating how a requirement will be verified and under what acceptance criteria.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id.

fields.name: system_element_id
fields.description: Foreign key to system_elements.id if verification applies to a specific element.

fields.name: verification_method_id
fields.description: Foreign key to verification_methods.id.

fields.name: verification_title
fields.description: Title of the verification case.

fields.name: acceptance_criteria
fields.description: Criteria that define successful verification.

fields.name: planned_phase_id
fields.description: Foreign key to lifecycle_phases.id for when verification is planned.

fields.name: responsible_org_id
fields.description: Foreign key to organizations.id for the verifying body.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Verification case status such as planned, in_progress, completed, passed, failed, or cancelled.

fields.name: due_date
fields.description: Planned completion date.

tables.table_purpose: Evidence records demonstrating the outcome of a verification case.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: verification_case_id
fields.description: Foreign key to verification_cases.id.

fields.name: evidence_document_id
fields.description: Foreign key to documents.id.

fields.name: evidence_type
fields.description: Type such as test_report, calculation_note, model_run, inspection_record, QA_record, commissioning_record, audit_report, or operational_monitoring_result.

fields.name: result_summary
fields.description: Summary of the outcome.

fields.name: result_status
fields.description: Result status such as pass, fail, partial, conditional_pass, or not_applicable.

fields.name: reviewed_by
fields.description: Reviewer name or reference.

fields.name: review_date
fields.description: Date of evidence review.

tables.table_purpose: Records used to validate that a requirement or solution satisfies the intended need or stakeholder expectation, not just technical conformance.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id.

fields.name: validation_question
fields.description: Question that frames the validation objective.

fields.name: stakeholder_basis
fields.description: Stakeholder need or use context being validated.

fields.name: method
fields.description: Validation method such as review, simulation, demonstration, user_acceptance, operational_trial, or safety_argument_review.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Validation status such as planned, completed, accepted, rejected, or deferred.

fields.name: evidence_document_id
fields.description: Foreign key to documents.id holding validation evidence.

tables.table_purpose: Formal change control table for proposed additions, modifications, deletions, reclassifications, and supersessions.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: cr_code

fields.name: title
fields.description: Short title of the change request.

fields.name: description

fields.name: reason_type
fields.description: Reason category such as new_regulation, design_change, site_data_update, safety_assessment_feedback, incident_lesson, stakeholder_request, or error_correction.

fields.name: initiator_org_id

fields.name: date_raised

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Priority of the change request.

fields.name: impact_summary

fields.name: status
fields.description: Change request status such as proposed, screening, under_review, approved, rejected, implemented, or closed.

fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: change_request_id
fields.description: Foreign key to change_requests.id.

fields.name: object_type
fields.description: Affected object type such as requirement, constraint, system_element, document, verification_case, or baseline.

fields.name: object_id
fields.description: Identifier of the affected object in its home table.

fields.name: proposed_action
fields.description: Action such as add, modify, delete, supersede, reclassify, or link.

fields.name: item_notes
fields.description: Optional details for the affected item.

tables.table_purpose: Immutable revision history snapshots for requirements.

fields.name: id

fields.name: requirement_id

fields.name: revision_no
fields.description: Revision identifier such as 0, 1, A, B, or semantic version notation depending on the chosen policy.

fields.name: revision_status
fields.description: Revision state such as draft, issued_for_review, approved, superseded, or withdrawn.

fields.name: statement_snapshot
fields.description: The exact requirement statement at this revision.

fields.name: rationale_snapshot
fields.description: Rationale text stored as it existed in this revision.

fields.name: change_request_id
fields.description: Foreign key to change_requests.id that triggered the revision.

fields.name: supersedes_revision_id
fields.description: Self-reference to requirement_revisions.id for the prior revision.

fields.name: approved_by
fields.description: Approver reference.

fields.name: approved_at
fields.description: Approval timestamp.

fields.name: baseline_id
fields.description: Foreign key to baselines.id if this revision is frozen in a baseline.

tables.table_purpose: Controlled snapshots of approved project states such as licensing baselines, design baselines, or reference configuration baselines.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: baseline_code
fields.description: Baseline code.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the baseline.

fields.name: description

fields.name: phase_id
fields.description: Foreign key to lifecycle_phases.id.

fields.name: frozen_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the baseline was frozen.

fields.name: approved_by
fields.description: Approving authority or reference.

fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: baseline_id

fields.name: object_type

fields.name: object_id
fields.description: Identifier of the included object.

fields.name: title

fields.name: decision_statement

fields.name: decision_type

fields.name: status

fields.name: decision_date

fields.name: decision_id
fields.description: Foreign key to decisions.id.

fields.name: requirement_id
fields.description: Foreign key to requirements.id.

fields.name: usage_type
fields.description: Usage type such as considered, driving, impacted, satisfied, interpreted, or waived.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: decision_id
fields.description: Foreign key to decisions.id.

fields.name: argument_type
fields.description: Type such as assumption, claim, evidence, uncertainty, tradeoff, or justification.

fields.name: argument_text
fields.description: Text of the argument.

fields.name: linked_document_id
fields.description: Foreign key to documents.id supporting the argument.

fields.name: confidence_level
fields.description: Qualitative confidence such as high, medium, low, or graded categories used by the organization.

tables.table_purpose: Formal record of conflicts or tradeoffs between requirements.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the conflict or tradeoff.

fields.name: identified_in_review_id

fields.name: resolution_decision_id
fields.description: Foreign key to decisions.id if resolved through a formal decision.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Status such as open, under_analysis, resolved, accepted_deviation, or closed.

fields.name: archived_flag

fields.name: linked_object_type

fields.name: linked_object_id

fields.name: link_type

tables.table_purpose: Associative table linking requirements to milestones, WBS items, and timing expectations.

fields.name: milestone_id

fields.name: wbs_item_id

fields.name: needed_by_date

fields.name: link_type

tables.table_purpose: Controlled list of organizations participating in the programme, project, licensing process, or support functions.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: org_code

fields.name: name

fields.name: org_type
fields.description: Type such as regulator, implementer, waste_producer, designer, supplier, QA, TSO, stakeholder_body, or contractor.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Internal primary key.

fields.name: object_type
fields.description: Object type such as requirement, system_element, document, baseline, verification_case, or change_request.

fields.name: object_id
fields.description: Identifier of the object in its home table.

fields.name: organization_id
fields.description: Foreign key to organizations.id.

fields.name: role_id
fields.description: Foreign key to roles.id.

fields.name: responsibility_type
fields.description: Specific responsibility type such as owner, author, reviewer, approver, verifier, validator, or informed_party.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Optional note on the responsibility assignment.

relationships.description: Each requirement belongs to one defined requirement level.
A requirement may have a primary lifecycle phase.
A requirement may be owned by one organization.
A requirement may reference a primary responsible role.
The requirement points to its currently approved revision snapshot.
Many attributes may belong to one requirement.
An attribute may point to the revision that established it.
A requirement may have many sources.
Each source link references one document.
Relationship source requirement.
Relationship target requirement.
Relationship may have been introduced through change control.
Self-referential hierarchy for functional architecture.
System element may be associated with one lifecycle phase.
Interface source element.
Interface target element.
Interface may be governed by a requirement.
One requirement may be allocated many times.
Allocations target system elements.
A constraint may originate from one document.
A constraint may be owned by one organization.
Many impacts may stem from one constraint.
A constraint impact may reference one requirement.
A constraint impact may reference one system element.
A requirement may have many verification cases.
A verification case may apply to a specific system element.
Each verification case uses one verification method.
Verification may be planned in one lifecycle phase.
Verification may be assigned to one organization.
One verification case may have many evidence records.
Each evidence record references one document.
A requirement may have many validation cases.
Validation may reference one evidence document.
A change request may be initiated by one organization.
A change request may affect many items.
One requirement may have many revisions.
A revision may be triggered by one change request.
Revision chain self-reference.
Revision may be frozen in a baseline.
A baseline may belong to a lifecycle phase.
A baseline may include many items.
A decision may be owned by one organization.
A decision may reference many requirements.
A requirement may be linked to many decisions.
A decision may have many structured arguments.
A decision argument may be supported by one document.
First requirement in a conflict pair.
Second requirement in a conflict pair.
Conflict may be resolved by a formal decision.
A document may be owned by one organization.
One document may be linked to many RMS objects.
A milestone may belong to a lifecycle phase.
Self-referential WBS hierarchy.
A WBS item may belong to a lifecycle phase.
A requirement may have many schedule links.
A schedule link may point to one milestone.
A schedule link may point to one WBS item.
One organization may have many responsibility assignments.
One role may be assigned many times.

requirement_levels.level_name: External High-Level Goals, Needs, and Expectations
Programme or System Goals and Principles
Production, Construction, Implementation, and Verification Specifications
Implemented Product Documentation and Compliance Evidence

implementation_notes: Polymorphic tables such as change_request_items, document_links, baseline_items, and responsibilities intentionally use object_type plus object_id instead of rigid foreign keys to every table. In a strict relational implementation, this may be enforced in application logic or replaced with multiple specialized link tables.
For SQL systems that do not handle json_array elegantly, arrays may be normalized into child tables.

For initial implementation, the most critical tables are requirements, requirement_levels, requirement_sources, requirement_relationships, system_elements, requirement_allocations, verification_cases, verification_evidence, change_requests, requirement_revisions, documents, and organizations.
The schema is suitable for conversion into PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MySQL, SQLite, NocoDB, or document-oriented systems with minor adaptation.
